question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary does not implement any rate limiting on probe invocations to invoke the healthcheck binary thousands of times per second, causing excessive os.Stat() syscalls that starve the pinger's heartbeat goroutine of I/O resources and cause /tmp/alive to become stale?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary does not verify the pinger's gRPC connection health before reporting liveness, allowing a pinger whose gRPC connection to the Hiero node has been severed (e.g., by a network partition) to appear alive while all Execute(client) calls are failing?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary does not check the pinger's error rate before reporting liveness, allowing a pinger that is logging 'transfer failed' for every tick to appear alive while an attacker is causing all transactions to fail via a man-in-the-middle attack on the gRPC channel?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary does not verify the pinger's toAccountID configuration before reporting readiness, causing the readiness probe to pass while the pinger is configured (via tampered HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID env var) to send all transactions to an attacker-controlled account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary does not verify the pinger's amountTinybar configuration before reporting readiness, causing the readiness probe to pass while the pinger is configured (via tampered HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR) to submit transactions with an extremely large amount that drains the operator account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary does not verify the pinger's interval configuration before reporting readiness, causing the readiness probe to pass while the pinger is configured (via tampered HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL) to submit transactions at an extremely high rate, flooding the Hiero network?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary does not verify the pinger's network configuration before reporting readiness, causing the readiness probe to pass while the pinger is configured (via tampered HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK) to submit transactions to a different network (e.g., mainnet instead of testnet)?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary does not verify the pinger's operatorKey before reporting readiness, causing the readiness probe to pass while the pinger is using the hardcoded default operator key (302e020100300506032b65700422042091132178e72057a1d7528025956fe39b0b847f200ab59b2fdd367017f3087137) that is publicly known, allowing anyone to sign transactions as the operator?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary does not verify the pinger's mirrorRest URL before reporting readiness, causing the readiness probe to pass while the pinger is configured (via tampered HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST) to fetch its network map from an attacker-controlled mirror node that returns malicious node endpoints?
